name: Betty Carter
description: American jazz singer, recording artist, songwriter (1929–1998)
abstract: Betty Carter (born Lillie Mae Jones; May 16, 1929 – September 26, 1998) was an American jazz singer known for her improvisational technique, scatting and other complex musical abilities that demonstrated her vocal talent and imaginative interpretation of lyrics and melodies. Vocalist Carmen McRae once remarked: "There's really only one j…

about_quotes.speaker: Christian McBride
about_quotes.evidence: had already performed and recorded with ... vocalist Betty Carter.
about_quotes.doc_title: Christian McBride interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: I said, “I’m down at Birdland with Betty Carter Saturday night.” ... So I called Prophet and he said, “Call Miles, Miles is getting ready to start a new band... I said, “I’m down at Birdland with Betty Carter Saturday night.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: We worked together. ... Whenever she would come in sometime and I would ask her to sing a song, Betty Carter would come up to the bandstand... No singer that I’ve ever known or worked with or recorded with, did.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Bryant
about_quotes.evidence: I did a recording session for Epic Records in conjunction with Betty Carter, Betty Carter, the singer, that was my first recording session and also her first, because we had the same manager, John Levy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Bryant interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: I had a contract and all the stuff, but I just walked away from it. ... At that time I believe you were already representing Ramsey Lewis, Betty Carter . . . Oh yes.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Winard Harper
about_quotes.evidence: me and the first band I played in with Betty Carter... I did it for four years... I always looked at it like a boot camp... she was tough but in a good way
about_quotes.doc_title: Winard Harper interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 10, 2014
